learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of the neuroanatomical basis of cognitive functions
apply the principles of cognitive neuroscience to child, adolescent and adult populations
understand how to operationalise cognitive mechanisms to enable their measurement and study
Communicate research findings from an independently-conducted project

assessments.due_date:
4/09/2026 3:00 pm

students are responsible for recruiting and collecting data from 3 participants other than themselves
assessments.task_description: Students will be allocated this tutorial time to collect data from three participants. It is expected that students should recruit friends/colleagues to come in to the lab and participate in the experiment, under the supervision of the tutor. Data collected will be collated and analysed for the report.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Students will formulate hypotheses and construct a programme to test face processing abilities. In the course of four tutorials, students will learn to use Matlab to display stimuli and collect response time data. The hypotheses and programme will be marked and corrected prior to data collection for the lab report.

NOTE: students will use the programme to test 3 participants, and describe the results, as part of their Report.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via drop box directly to tutor.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
On-line, in tutorial class time

Quiz 1 (10%): in-class open book, 20 multiple choice questions, lectures 1-3

Quiz 2 (10%): in-class open book, 20 multiple choice questions, lecture 4-6 

Quiz 3 (20%): in-class open book 40 questions multiple choice lectures 7-12
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Lab report - Gaze cueing- part a: Results and Discussion
assessments.task_description:
In-class assignment: Using the Matlab programme constructed in the first half of the course, the hypotheses generated, and the background literature provided, in previous tutorials students will collect, process and analyse behavioural data (reaction times) from the participants. In tutorial of week 12, they will write up their analyses and submit their lab report.

1.500 words total
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
